response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with deep, varied engineering experience and a genuine developer-tooling streak (test runners, assertion libraries, CI infrastructure, MCP code generators), but the role is fundamentally a people-management position and the resume shows no formal people-management or organizational leadership experience. Technical leadership signals are informal (workshops, dojos, founding an open-source project) rather than accountable team leadership, and there is no evidence of roadmap ownership, performance management, or leading organizational change at the scale this Level II manager role requires.
strengths: 15+ years of software development across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Authentic developer-tools experience: test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD setup, device orchestration tooling, MCP code generators
Strong engineering-culture contributions: TDD advocacy, workshops, code katas, and conference talks
concerns: No formal people-management experience against a hard requirement of 2+ years
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, roadmaps, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform exposure is limited (one iOS/Apple TV stint in 2015-2016) versus the role's mobile-ecosystem focus

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure background
Informal rather than accountable technical leadership
